title: Queens Backflow Reinspection Guide
heroTitle: How to Pass Backflow Reinspection in Queens
heroSubtitle: Clear, documented sequence to resolve violations and pass reinspection without avoidable delays.
metaDescription: Queens backflow reinspection guide for commercial properties. Learn checklist, documentation, and scheduling best practices.

sections.title: Reinspection Is a Documentation Test Too
sections.content: Most reinspection setbacks are not technical - they are missing paperwork, unclear repair records, or weak handoffs.

sections.title: Pre-Reinspection Checklist
sections.content: Complete this before any reinspection date is locked.

sections.title: How to Avoid Repeat Notices
sections.content: Convert your reinspection process into a repeatable annual workflow.
items: Set annual test month by site
Track devices in one master sheet

calloutContent: We can coordinate your team and vendors so reinspection closes cleanly and compliance stays on schedule.

faqs.question: What is the #1 reason reinspection fails?
faqs.answer: Incomplete documentation handoff between repair, testing, and filing teams.

faqs.question: Should I keep separate files by vendor?
faqs.answer: No. Keep one property-level packet so any inspector response is immediate.

faqs.question: Can a passing test still lead to issues?
faqs.answer: Yes, if records are incomplete or filed incorrectly.

faqs.question: How do I shorten closeout time?
faqs.answer: Book retest at the same time as repair dispatch and finalize packet same day.
